reasoning: The current position can be calculated as initial_position + velocity * time. We can test this by changing the velocity and observing the effect on position.

Increasing the velocity resulted in a proportional increase in the position, supporting the hypothesis that position = initial_position + velocity * time. To confirm, I will now increase the time and observe the change in position.
The position has increased proportionally with both velocity and time, confirming the equation position = initial_position + velocity * time. No further actions are needed to gather evidence.
